title: oxalis tacorensis
intro: Oxalis tacorensis, also known as the forest sorrel or wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). Traditionally, Oxalis tacorensis has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in vitamins A and C, and its leaves have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Oxalis tacorensis can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing its leaves in hot water, or used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves.
